title: CSS Selectors and Specificity Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential hierarchy and scoring systems for styling your web content using CSS.

questions.question: Which of these selectors has the MOST specificity (points)?
Which CSS property on a parent element allows its font styles to be inherited by its children?
In a CSS rule (e.g., 'nav ul li a'), how many points does a Type selector (like 'li') contribute to the overall specificity?
What happens if two CSS rules have the EXACT same specificity and target the same element?
options: The browser chooses the first one.
The rule that appears LATER (lower down) in the CSS file 'wins'.

questions.question: What is the primary danger of using '!important' in your CSS rules?
options: It makes the site load much slower.
It makes debugging very difficult because it overrides almost all other specificity rules.
It is not supported in modern browsers.
